languages.text: {n}Следующий на очереди старик.{/n} "Проповедник Клиофан. Изначально определенный на служение в корабельный реликварий, отринул роскошество часовни в пользу вечного паломничества по палубам, куда не ступала пята служителя Экклезиархии. Своим примером и словом он воодушевлял и наставлял отверженных и забытых. Тысячи душ, что иначе утратили бы веру и канули в ересь, познали слово Императора. Но хватило лишь одной, чтобы оборвать его речи — безумец накинулся на него во время проповеди и выдрал голосовой имплант голыми руками, прежде чем толпа растерзала его".

{n}Next is the old man.{/n} "Preacher Cleophanes. Originally assigned to serve in the ship's reliquary, he rejected the luxury of the chapel in favour of an eternal pilgrimage on the decks where no servant of the Ecclesiarchy had ever set foot. Through his example and word, he encouraged and instructed the outcast and forgotten. Thousands of souls who would otherwise have lost their faith and fallen into heresy, came to know the word of the Emperor. But it only took one to end Cleophanes's preaching for good — a madman attacked him during a sermon, managing to rip out the preacher's vocal implant before the crowd tore him apart."
languages.original_text: {n}Следующий на очереди старик.{/n} "Проповедник Клиофан. languages.text: {n}次は老人。{/n} 「クレオファネス神父。本来は艦内の聖遺物庫で奉仕するはずだったが、教会の豪華さを捨てて、聖職者が足を踏み入れたことのない甲板での永遠の巡礼を選んだ。彼の模範と教えによって、捨てられた者、忘れられた者たちは励まされ、導かれた。そうでなければ信仰を失い異端へと堕ちていたであろう数千の魂が、皇帝の教えを知ることになった。しかし、クレオファネスの説教を永遠に終わらせるには、たった一人しかいなかった。狂人によって説教中に襲われ、群衆に引き裂かれる前に、彼の声のインプラントが引き抜かれたのだ」
languages.original_text: {n}Next is the old man.{/n} "Preacher Cleophanes. Originally assigned to serve in the ship's reliquary, he rejected the luxury of the chapel in favour of an eternal pilgrimage on the decks where no servant of the Ecclesiarchy had ever set foot. Through his example and word, he encouraged and instructed the outcast and forgotten. Thousands of souls who would otherwise have lost their faith and fallen into heresy, came to know the word of the Emperor. But it only took one to end Cleophanes's preaching for good — a madman attacked him during a sermon, managing to rip out the preacher's vocal implant before the crowd tore him apart."

languages.text: {n}接下来是那个老人。{/n}“布道士克莱奥法内斯。他最初被分配到舰上的圣物室服役，但他拒绝了圣堂的奢华，转而在国教神职人员从未涉足过的甲板上永恒朝圣。他言传身教，鼓励并教导那些被放逐，被遗忘的人。成千上万本会丧失信仰、堕入异端泥潭的灵魂，得以聆听帝皇的真言。然而，要终结克莱奥法内斯的布道生涯，仅仅需要一个人——某次布道时，一个疯子袭击了他，硬生生扯出了他的发声植入物，这疯子随后便被人群撕成碎片。”
languages.original_text: {n}Next is the old man.{/n} "Preacher Cleophanes. Originally assigned to serve in the ship's reliquary, he rejected the luxury of the chapel in favour of an eternal pilgrimage on the decks where no servant of the Ecclesiarchy had ever set foot. Through his example and word, he encouraged and instructed the outcast and forgotten. Thousands of souls who would otherwise have lost their faith and fallen into heresy, came to know the word of the Emperor. But it only took one to end Cleophanes's preaching for good — a madman attacked him during a sermon, managing to rip out the preacher's vocal implant before the crowd tore him apart."

languages.text: {n}El siguiente es el anciano.{/n} «El predicador Cleophanes. Originalmente, se asignó al servicio del relicario de la nave, pero rechazó los lujos de la capilla en favor de un peregrinaje eterno en las cubiertas a las que ningún sirviente de la Eclesiarquía se había adentrado. Su ejemplo y sus palabras animaron y educaron a los parias y los olvidados. Miles de almas que habrían perdido la fe y caído en la herejía volvieron al camino del Emperador gracias a él... pero solo hizo falta uno para poner fin a sus enseñanzas: un loco lo atacó durante un sermón y consiguió arrancarle el implante vocal antes de que la muchedumbre lo desmembrara».
languages.original_text: {n}Next is the old man.{/n} "Preacher Cleophanes. Originally assigned to serve in the ship's reliquary, he rejected the luxury of the chapel in favour of an eternal pilgrimage on the decks where no servant of the Ecclesiarchy had ever set foot. Through his example and word, he encouraged and instructed the outcast and forgotten. Thousands of souls who would otherwise have lost their faith and fallen into heresy, came to know the word of the Emperor. But it only took one to end Cleophanes's preaching for good — a madman attacked him during a sermon, managing to rip out the preacher's vocal implant before the crowd tore him apart."

traits.locale_text: {n}El siguiente es el anciano.{/n} «El predicador Cleophanes. Originalmente, se asignó al servicio del relicario de la nave, pero rechazó los lujos de la capilla en favor de un peregrinaje eterno en las cubiertas a las que ningún sirviente de la Eclesiarquía se había adentrado. Su ejemplo y sus palabras animaron y educaron a los parias y los olvidados. Miles de almas que habrían perdido la fe y caído en la herejía volvieron al camino del Emperador gracias a él... pero solo hizo falta uno para poner fin a sus enseñanzas: un loco lo atacó durante un sermón y consiguió arrancarle el implante vocal antes de que la muchedumbre lo desmembrara».

languages.text: {n}Er fährt mit dem alten Mann fort.{/n} "Prediger Cleophanes. War ursprünglich dem Reliquiar des Schiffs zugeteilt, ehe er dem Luxus der Kapelle zugunsten einer ewigen Pilgerschaft auf den Decks entsagte, die noch kein Diener der Ekklesiarchie betreten hatte. Sein Beispiel und seine Worte haben die Verstoßenen und Vergessenen ermutigt und geleitet. Tausende von Seelen, die sonst ihren Glauben verloren hätten und der Ketzerei verfallen wären, erfuhren so das Wort des Imperators. Ein Mann reichte jedoch aus, um Cleophanes für immer verstummen zu lassen ... ein Wahnsinniger riss ihm während einer Predigt sein Vokalimplantat aus dem Leib, ehe er selbst von der Menge getötet wurde."
languages.original_text: {n}Next is the old man.{/n} "Preacher Cleophanes. Originally assigned to serve in the ship's reliquary, he rejected the luxury of the chapel in favour of an eternal pilgrimage on the decks where no servant of the Ecclesiarchy had ever set foot. Through his example and word, he encouraged and instructed the outcast and forgotten. Thousands of souls who would otherwise have lost their faith and fallen into heresy, came to know the word of the Emperor. But it only took one to end Cleophanes's preaching for good — a madman attacked him during a sermon, managing to rip out the preacher's vocal implant before the crowd tore him apart."

traits.locale_text: {n}Er fährt mit dem alten Mann fort.{/n} "Prediger Cleophanes. War ursprünglich dem Reliquiar des Schiffs zugeteilt, ehe er dem Luxus der Kapelle zugunsten einer ewigen Pilgerschaft auf den Decks entsagte, die noch kein Diener der Ekklesiarchie betreten hatte. Sein Beispiel und seine Worte haben die Verstoßenen und Vergessenen ermutigt und geleitet. Tausende von Seelen, die sonst ihren Glauben verloren hätten und der Ketzerei verfallen wären, erfuhren so das Wort des Imperators. Ein Mann reichte jedoch aus, um Cleophanes für immer verstummen zu lassen ... ein Wahnsinniger riss ihm während einer Predigt sein Vokalimplantat aus dem Leib, ehe er selbst von der Menge getötet wurde."

languages.text: {n}Vient ensuite le vieil homme.{/n} « Le prêcheur Cleophanes. À l'origine, il était affecté au reliquaire du vaisseau, mais il a rejeté le luxe de la chapelle pour partir en pèlerinage perpétuel sur les ponts où aucun autre serviteur de l'Ecclésiarchie n'avait mis les pieds avant lui. À travers son exemple et ses prédications, il encourageait et instruisait les parias et les oubliés. Il a ainsi fait connaître la parole de l'Empereur à des milliers d'âmes qui, sans lui, auraient perdu la foi et succombé à l'hérésie. Hélas, il a suffi d'une personne pour mettre fin aux bonnes œuvres de Cleophanes : lors d'un sermon, un fou l'a attaqué et a réussi à arracher son implant vocal avant que la foule ne l'écharpe. »
languages.original_text: {n}Next is the old man.{/n} "Preacher Cleophanes. Originally assigned to serve in the ship's reliquary, he rejected the luxury of the chapel in favour of an eternal pilgrimage on the decks where no servant of the Ecclesiarchy had ever set foot. Through his example and word, he encouraged and instructed the outcast and forgotten. Thousands of souls who would otherwise have lost their faith and fallen into heresy, came to know the word of the Emperor. But it only took one to end Cleophanes's preaching for good — a madman attacked him during a sermon, managing to rip out the preacher's vocal implant before the crowd tore him apart."

traits.locale_text: {n}Vient ensuite le vieil homme.{/n} « Le prêcheur Cleophanes. À l'origine, il était affecté au reliquaire du vaisseau, mais il a rejeté le luxe de la chapelle pour partir en pèlerinage perpétuel sur les ponts où aucun autre serviteur de l'Ecclésiarchie n'avait mis les pieds avant lui. Il a ainsi fait connaître la parole de l'Empereur à des milliers d'âmes qui, sans lui, auraient perdu la foi et succombé à l'hérésie. Hélas, il a suffi d'une personne pour mettre fin aux bonnes œuvres de Cleophanes : lors d'un sermon, un fou l'a attaqué et a réussi à arracher son implant vocal avant que la foule ne l'écharpe. »

languages.text: {n}Sırada ihtiyarlamış adam var.{/n} "Vaiz Cleophanes. Aslen geminin kutsal emanet odasında hizmet etmek üzere görevlendirilmiş ancak şapelin sunduğu lüks hayatı reddederek hiçbir Ekleşiarşi hizmetkârının ayak basmadığı güvertelerde sonsuz bir hac yolculuğunu tercih etmiş. Örnek davranışlarıyla ve sözleriyle dışlanmışları ve unutulmuşları cesaretlendirip eğitmiş. Aksi takdirde inançlarını kaybedip kâfirliğe yenik düşecek olan binlerce ruh, İmparator'un sözlerini ondan öğrenmiş. Ancak Cleophanes'in vaazlarının sona ermesi için bir kişi yeterliymiş. Vaaz sırasında delinin teki ona saldırmış, vaizin ses implantını söküp almayı başarmış. Saldırgan, sonrasında kalabalığın elinde paramparça olmuş."
languages.original_text: {n}Next is the old man.{/n} "Preacher Cleophanes. Originally assigned to serve in the ship's reliquary, he rejected the luxury of the chapel in favour of an eternal pilgrimage on the decks where no servant of the Ecclesiarchy had ever set foot. Through his example and word, he encouraged and instructed the outcast and forgotten. Thousands of souls who would otherwise have lost their faith and fallen into heresy, came to know the word of the Emperor. But it only took one to end Cleophanes's preaching for good — a madman attacked him during a sermon, managing to rip out the preacher's vocal implant before the crowd tore him apart."
